title: OpenRouter API configuration and model selection in pi-mono/pi-ai
question: How to add an OpenRouter API key to a Pi coding agent installation alongside an existing Anthropic key, and how to select OpenRouter-hosted models?
findings_summary:
OpenRouter is a first-class built-in provider in both pi-ai and the pi coding agent, requiring no extension wiring. Configuration requires either setting the OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable or adding an openrouter entry to ~/.pi/agent/auth.json (file permissions 0600) with key types supporting literal values, environment variable references, or shell commands. The credential resolution order is (1) CLI --api-key flag, (2) auth.json entry, (3) environment variable, (4) custom provider apiKey from models.json.

OpenRouter models are accessed via their native vendor/model namespace (e.g., anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6) with no pi-invented prefix. Model selection occurs through CLI flags --provider openrouter --model <id>, the shorthand --model openrouter/<id>, or the interactive /model picker which displays only providers with resolvable credentials. The transport layer uses openai-completions via the openai npm SDK, with baseUrl https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 and Authorization: Bearer header set automatically.

For Claude-via-OpenRouter, pi-ai automatically injects cache_control: {type: ephemeral} on the last text block and uses reasoning: {effort} format instead of reasoning_effort. The model catalog in dist/models.generated.js is a static build-time snapshot; models released after pi-ai publish may be added via ~/.pi/agent/models.json using either Option A (override the built-in openrouter provider) or Option B (register a distinct provider namespace). Tool-forced output with Claude-via-OpenRouter is not explicitly documented as working or failing; empirical testing is required before relying on forced-tool-use behavior such as that used by pi-behavior-monitors. HTTP-Referer and X-Title headers are not sent by default but can be added via models.json header override. Extended cache retention (PI_CACHE_RETENTION=long) does not apply to OpenRouter routes, only to direct Anthropic and OpenAI calls.
stale_conditions: @mariozechner/pi-ai version bumped past 0.63.1 (model catalog may change)
badlogic/pi-mono main branch updated with breaking changes to auth.json or provider resolution order
OpenRouter API endpoint URL or auth header format changes
Issue #2921 or #2955 status changes regarding custom model merging behavior

citations.label: OpenRouter-specific code paths cache-control and reasoning format
